title: Fix 5 standing failures that are stale tests (#3, #4, #5, #8, #14 — triage bucket A)
intent: Batch A of the api-verify standing-failures triage. Five residual reds are STALE TESTS — the product moved and the tests didn't. #3/#4/#5 the IBG solve-from-landmarks endpoint gained Depends(get_current_principal) in IBG-2B, so unauthenticated TestClient POSTs now 401; #8 a permissive-gate test approves before attempting bypass, so the bypass counter never increments; #14 a lifecycle fixture used a synthetic translator_id that 7C's registry validation rejects. Each was re-grounded against current code before fixing. Test-only; no production code touched, no behavior change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (#3/#4/#5) test_morphology_spine_e2e.py: the three failing tests POST to /api/body/solve-from-landmarks (a paid-tier endpoint that gained Depends(get_current_principal) in IBG-2B) without auth, so TestClient got 401. Added a module-level _AUTH_HEADERS = {'x-user-role':'operator','x-user-id':'test_user_123'} (the same legacy header-auth convention the passing test_body_solver_integration.py uses) and passed headers=_AUTH_HEADERS to the three solve POSTs. #5 additionally had NO status guard before ibg_data['outline_points'] (so the 401 body caused KeyError, not a clear failure); added the assert solve_response.status_code == 200 guard the sibling tests already have. (#8) test_ibg_intake_gate.py::test_permissive_gate_tolerates_bypass_attempts: reordered so the two machine bypass attempts (set_review_required(False,'system:bot')) run BEFORE the human approval. set_review_required only increments _bypass_attempt_count when _review_required is still True; approving first cleared the flag, so the attempts no-op'd and the counter stayed 0 (REVIEW_BYPASS_DETECTED never fired). Attempting pre-approval makes the counter reach 2, so the default gate rejects and the permissive gate (allow=2) accepts. (#14) test_operation_capability_registry.py: the shared _create_lifecycle_request helper hardcoded translator_id='test_translator', which 7C validate_translator_registry rejects (not in TRANSLATOR_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY) -> translator stage RED. Changed to 'dxf_r12' (category=translator, output_class=dxf, execution_state=validation_only not disabled, supported_operations include nut_slot/drilling) so test_registered_operation_works gets green/yellow.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: All five hypotheses were re-verified against current code (the earlier #1/#2 diagnosis had proven wrong, so no triage row was trusted). #3/#4/#5 share one root cause (the IBG-2B auth gate, commit 70a0d3ee) and the exact header convention was copied from a PASSING test, not guessed. #8's reorder was validated against the set_review_required increment guard (`if self._review_required and not required`). #14's replacement id was confirmed present and valid in TRANSLATOR_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY, and no sibling test depends on 'test_translator' being rejected (test_unregistered_operation_returns_red is red because of the OPERATION, not the translator). All test-only; zero product behavior change.

commands_run: services/api venv: pytest tests/mrp_spine_verification/test_morphology_spine_e2e.py -> 10 passed, 1 skipped (#3/#4/#5 now authenticate and 200; the skip is a real-artifact test with no PDF present; sibling tests unaffected)
pytest tests/test_ibg_intake_gate.py tests/cam/test_operation_capability_registry.py -> 48 passed (#8 reorder + #14 dxf_r12; every sibling in both files still green)
py_compile of all 3 edited test files -> OK
note: full api-verify is an Ubuntu-CI witness (fastapi 0.137 pin); local venv is fastapi 0.121.1

files.intent: #3/#4/#5: send IBG-2B header-auth on the three solve-from-landmarks POSTs; add #5's missing 200 status guard
#8: attempt bypasses before approval so the bypass counter actually increments
#14: use a registered translator id (dxf_r12) instead of the synthetic test_translator the registry rejects
